# Extração: 33.05-Presentation for CA Call_9-29-20

- **Fonte:** 33.05-Presentation for CA Call_9-29-20.docx
- **Tipo:** DOCX
- **Tamanho:** 13.5 KB

---

Hockey stick growth isn’t always the best type of scaling. 

What happens when the stick snaps in half?



You can have a “real” company without having a bloated company. 

Payroll, hiring, redundancies, managing for profit. 



Revenue is a vanity metric. 

Profit is a lot better. 



Pay attention to refund rates. 

The Pregnancy Model (sort of): 30 Days of Pleasure can Turn into 9 Months of Indigestion.



If you want to build a “real” company than it needs to be a compliant company. 

California District Attorneys, FDA/FTC/, Sleeping At Night, Truth About Jail Time. Plus, customer service, refunds, managing for chargebacks, etc. 



You can be pretty compliant and still sell a lot of stuff. 



The hidden money-makers: 

Email monetization, partials and declines, call center, ad words, holiday sales, selling repeat customers, and more. 



The Stuff that Fucks You (Ow!)

Inventory management. Merchant Processing. Did I mention bloat? Did I mention compliance? Oh I did? Well I’m mentioning them again. 



Who Gives a Fuck? (You Do!)

Ultimately nobody is going to care more about your offer, or your business, than you. You need to be the CEO, you need to be the affiliate manager, you need to be the customer service supervisor, and pretty much everything else. Abdication vs. Delegation and the eMyth.



The Power of Focus

You can usually double, triple, or quadruple down on your existing offer AND it makes more sense to do that than to start a new offer or funnel. 

 

Your Name is not Urk!

We don’t know who actually invented the wheel, but I feel like their name could have been Urk. Here’s the thing, you’re not Urk. You didn’t invent the wheel and you don’t need to invent a new business model. Follow what works!
